title: Fractions of a Whole
essential_question: How can a fraction name part of a whole?
strategies: count total equal parts for denominator

pretest.question: A circle is divided into 4 equal parts. 3 parts are shaded. What fraction is shaded?
pretest.explanation: 3 shaded parts out of 4 equal parts = 3/4.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson 8.4 'Fractions of a Whole' pp.325-328
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic B — Fractions a/b as a Iterations of 1/b
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: A rectangle is divided into 6 equal parts. 2 parts are shaded. What fraction is shaded?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson 8.4 'Fractions of a Whole' pp.325-328
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic B — Fractions a/b as a Iterations of 1/b
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: Which fraction has 5 as the numerator?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson 8.4 'Fractions of a Whole' pp.325-328
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic B — Fractions a/b as a Iterations of 1/b

pretest.question: Which fraction is equal to 1 whole?
pretest.explanation: When the numerator equals the denominator, the fraction equals 1 whole. 6/6 = 1.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson 8.4 'Fractions of a Whole' pp.325-328
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic B — Fractions a/b as a Iterations of 1/b
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Naming Fractions
learn.content: To name a fraction: count the shaded (or chosen) parts — that is the numerator (top). Count the total equal parts — that is the denominator (bottom). Example: 3 out of 4 parts shaded = 3/4.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson 8.4 'Fractions of a Whole' pp.325-328
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic B — Fractions a/b as a Iterations of 1/b
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: More Than a Unit Fraction
learn.content: A unit fraction has 1 as the numerator (like 1/4). When more than 1 part is shaded, the numerator is greater than 1 (like 2/4 or 3/4).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson 8.4 'Fractions of a Whole' pp.325-328
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic B — Fractions a/b as a Iterations of 1/b
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: The Whole as a Fraction
learn.content: If ALL parts are shaded, the fraction equals the whole. 4/4 = 1 whole, 6/6 = 1 whole, 8/8 = 1 whole.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson 8.4 'Fractions of a Whole' pp.325-328
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic B — Fractions a/b as a Iterations of 1/b
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 분자의 의미 — '몇 조각 가졌나'
learn.content: 분수 a/b에서 분자 a는 '단위분수 1/b를 몇 개 모았는지'를 알려줌. 분모 b: 전체를 몇 등분 / 분자 a: 그 중 몇 조각. 예: 3/4 → 4등분 중 3조각 (1/4 + 1/4 + 1/4). 예: 5/8 → 8등분 중 5조각. 🔍 빠른 확인: 분자=색칠한 조각 수, 분모=전체 조각 수.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson 8.4 'Fractions of a Whole' pp.325-328
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic B — Fractions a/b as a Iterations of 1/b
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 분수 a/b 만들기 4단계 루틴
learn.content: 단계 1) 전체(whole) 정하기. 단계 2) b개의 같은 부분으로 나누기. 단계 3) a개를 색칠하기. 단계 4) a/b로 적기. 예: 8조각 피자에서 5조각 먹음 → 5/8 먹음, 3/8 남음. 흔한 실수 (M02): 같은 크기 조건 무시 — 항상 균등 분할 검증.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson 8.4 'Fractions of a Whole' pp.325-328
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic B — Fractions a/b as a Iterations of 1/b
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 먹은 + 남은 = 1 (전체)
learn.content: 분수에서 색칠한 부분과 남은 부분을 합하면 항상 전체(b/b = 1). 예: 3/4 색칠 → 1/4 남음 (3/4 + 1/4 = 4/4 = 1). 예: 5/8 색칠 → 3/8 남음 (5/8 + 3/8 = 8/8 = 1). 🔍 검산: 색칠 분자 + 남은 분자 = 분모. 맞으면 OK. 흔한 실수 (M04): 전체를 잊고 분자끼리만 비교 — 분모(전체)를 항상 확인.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson 8.4 'Fractions of a Whole' pp.325-328
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic B — Fractions a/b as a Iterations of 1/b
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 단위분수의 반복 — a/b = a × (1/b)
learn.content: 분수 a/b는 단위분수 1/b를 a번 모은 것.   2/3 = 1/3 + 1/3 (1/3을 2번)   4/5 = 1/5 + 1/5 + 1/5 + 1/5 (1/5을 4번) 수직선/막대모델에서 1/b 단위로 a칸 이동 → a/b 위치. 🔍 활용: 분자가 분모와 같으면 a/b = b/b = 1 (전체).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson 8.4 'Fractions of a Whole' pp.325-328
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic B — Fractions a/b as a Iterations of 1/b
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 어림 검산 — 1/2와 비교
learn.content: 분수가 합리적인지 1/2 기준으로 어림:   분자 < 분모/2 → 1/2보다 작음 (예: 3/8, 분모/2=4)   분자 = 분모/2 → 정확히 1/2 (예: 4/8)   분자 > 분모/2 → 1/2보다 큼 (예: 5/8) 예: 5/6은 분모/2=3보다 큰 5 → 1/2보다 큼 ✓. 흔한 실수 (M03): '6분의 5'를 '5분의 6'으로 거꾸로 읽음.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson 8.4 'Fractions of a Whole' pp.325-328
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic B — Fractions a/b as a Iterations of 1/b
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: A rectangle is divided into 6 equal parts. 4 parts are shaded. What fraction is shaded?
try.explanation: Numerator = shaded parts (4); denominator = total equal parts (6) → 4/6.
hints: Top number = shaded.
Bottom number = total parts.

feedback.incorrect: 4 shaded out of 6 total = 4/6.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson 8.4 'Fractions of a Whole' pp.325-328
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic B — Fractions a/b as a Iterations of 1/b
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Which fraction equals 1 whole?
try.explanation: When numerator = denominator, the fraction equals 1 whole. 7/7 = 1.
hints: All parts shaded means whole.

feedback.incorrect: When top = bottom, fraction = 1. So 7/7=1.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson 8.4 'Fractions of a Whole' pp.325-328
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic B — Fractions a/b as a Iterations of 1/b

try.question: A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. Lily ate 3 slices. What fraction did she eat, and what fraction is left?
hints: Eaten = 3/8.
Left = 8 − 3 = 5 parts → 5/8.

feedback.incorrect: 3 of 8 = 3/8 eaten; 5 of 8 = 5/8 left.

practice_r2.question: Which fraction is greater than 1/2?
practice_r2.explanation: 3/4 means 3 out of 4 parts, which is more than half (2 out of 4). The others are less than or equal to 1/2.
hints: Think about what fractions of a whole means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is C. Remember how fractions of a whole works.

practice_r2.question: Which is read as 'three-fifths'?
practice_r2.explanation: Korean reads denominator first ('5분의'), numerator second ('3'). English: 3/5 = three-fifths (numerator first).
hints: Numerator on top.
3 over 5 = three-fifths.

feedback.incorrect: Three-fifths = 3 on top, 5 on bottom = 3/5.

practice_r2.question: Find the difference: 600 − 247.
practice_r2.explanation: 600−247: 0−7 borrow → 10−7=3; tens 9−4=5 (after lending across zero); hundreds 5−2=3. Result: 353.
hints: Borrow across the zero.

practice_r3.question: A rectangle is divided into 8 equal parts. Lily shades more than 4/8 but fewer than 7/8. Which could be the fraction she shaded?
practice_r3.explanation: More than 4/8 and fewer than 7/8 → 5/8 or 6/8. 5/8 is the matching choice.
hints: Think about what fractions of a whole means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is C. Remember how fractions of a whole works.

practice_r3.question: Two shapes are each divided into equal parts. Shape A has 3/4 shaded. Shape B has 3/6 shaded. Which statement is true?
choices: Shape A and Shape B have the same fraction shaded
Shape A has a greater fraction shaded than Shape B
Shape B has a greater fraction shaded than Shape A
practice_r3.explanation: 3/4 = 0.75 and 3/6 = 0.5, so Shape A has a greater fraction shaded.
hints: Think about what fractions of a whole means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how fractions of a whole works.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson 8.4 'Fractions of a Whole' pp.325-328
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic B — Fractions a/b as a Iterations of 1/b
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Jake says 2/4 and 4/8 are different amounts. Is he correct?
choices: Yes, because the numbers are different
No, both fractions name the same amount
practice_r3.explanation: 2/4 = 1/2 and 4/8 = 1/2. They name the same amount even though the numbers look different.
hints: Think about what fractions of a whole means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is C. Remember how fractions of a whole works.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson 8.4 'Fractions of a Whole' pp.325-328
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic B — Fractions a/b as a Iterations of 1/b
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Mark cut a pizza into 8 equal slices. He ate 3 slices and gave 2 slices to his friend. What fraction of the pizza is left?
hints: Add eaten and given first.

feedback.incorrect: 5 slices used, 3 slices left → 3/8.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson 8.4 'Fractions of a Whole' pp.325-328
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic B — Fractions a/b as a Iterations of 1/b

practice_r3.question: Two shapes are each divided into 6 equal parts. Shape A has 2 parts shaded; Shape B has 4 parts shaded. Which statement is true?
choices: A. Shape A shows 2/6, Shape B shows 4/6 — both have the same denominator.
B. Shape A shows 6/2 and Shape B shows 6/4.
C. Both shapes show the same fraction.
practice_r3.explanation: Both shapes are divided into 6 parts (same denominator). A: 2 shaded → 2/6. B: 4 shaded → 4/6.
hints: Same total parts → same denominator.
Numerator changes with shaded count.

feedback.incorrect: Same denominator (6); numerators differ (2 vs 4).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson 8.4 'Fractions of a Whole' pp.325-328
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic B — Fractions a/b as a Iterations of 1/b
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

vertical_alignment.prerequisite: G3 U8 L3 — 단위분수 1/b: 전체를 b등분한 것 중 한 부분 (3.NF.A.1)
